@summary.text: A breadcrumb is a secondary navigation scheme that reveals the user's location in a website or web application.

@overview.text:
- Breadcrumbs provide a trail for users to follow back to the starting or entry point.
 - They help users understand their current location within the site hierarchy.
 - Follows WCAG 2.1 guidelines for accessible navigation landmarks.

customElement.name: Breadcrumb

members.name: label
members.description: Accessible label for the breadcrumb navigation landmark.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Accessible label for the breadcrumb navigation landmark.

customElement.summary: A breadcrumb is a secondary navigation scheme that reveals the user's location in a website or web application.

members.name: href
members.description: Hyperlink to navigate to on click.

members.name: target
members.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

members.name: active
members.description: Indicates if this is the active (current) breadcrumb item.

members.name: separator
members.description: Custom separator character. If not provided, default "/" is used.

@summary.text: Buttons help people initiate actions, from sending an email, to sharing a document, to liking a post.

@overview.text: Buttons are clickable elements that are used to trigger actions. They communicate calls to action to the user and allow users to interact with pages in a variety of ways. Button labels express what action will occur when the user interacts with it.

@fires.text: click - Dispatched when the button is clicked.

members.name: iconAlign
members.description:
Icon alignment.
Possible values are `"start"`, `"end"`. Defaults to `"end"`.

members.name: type
members.description: Type is preset of color and variant. Type will be only applied.

members.name: variant
members.description:
The visual style of the button.

 Possible variant values:
`"filled"` is a filled button.
`"outlined"` is an outlined button.
`"text"` is a transparent button.
`"tonal"` is a light color button.
`"elevated"` is elevated button

members.name: color
members.description: Defines the primary color of the button. This can be set to predefined color names to apply specific color themes.

members.name: size
members.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: softDisabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button and the button is visually styled as disabled. But the button is still focusable. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: disabledReason
members.description: If button is disabled, the reason why it is disabled.

members.name: href
members.description: Hyperlink to navigate to on click.

members.name: target
members.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

members.name: throttleDelay
members.description: Sets the delay for throttle in milliseconds. Defaults to 200 milliseconds.

members.name: isPressed
members.description: States

events.description: Dispatched when the button is clicked.
events.name: click

@summary.text: A vertical bar chart that follows Material Design 3 color and spacing tokens.

@example.text:
```html
<wc-chart-bar width="520" height="320"></wc-chart-bar>
<script>
  document.querySelector('wc-chart-bar').data = [
    { name: 'apples', label: 'Apples', value: 20 },
    { name: 'bananas', label: 'Bananas', value: 35 },
    { name: 'cherries', label: 'Cherries', value: 15 },
  ];
</script>
```

customElement.name: ChartBar

members.name: width
members.description: Width of the chart in pixels.

members.name: height
members.description: Height of the chart in pixels.

members.name: margin
members.description: Margin around the chart drawing area.

members.name: data
members.description: Chart data array. Each item should have name, value, and optional label and color.

members.name: showValues
members.description: Whether to render value labels above bars.

attributes.description: Width of the chart in pixels.

@summary.text: A donut chart is a circular chart with a blank center. The area in the center can be used to display information.

@example.text:
```html
<wc-chart-donut width="400" label="Total"></wc-chart-donut>
<script>
  document.querySelector('wc-chart-donut').data = [
    { name: 'A', value: 30, label: 'Category A' },
    { name: 'B', value: 50, label: 'Category B' },
    { name: 'C', value: 20, label: 'Category C' },
  ];
</script>
```

customElement.name: ChartDonut

members.description: Margin around the chart.

members.name: showLabels
members.description: Whether to show labels outside the chart.

members.name: data
members.description: Chart data array. Each item should have name, value, and optional label and color.

members.name: label
members.description: Label displayed in the center of the donut.

@summary.text: Captures boolean input with an optional indeterminate mode.

@overview.text:
Checkboxes allow users to select one or more items from a set. Checkboxes can turn an option on or off.

Material Design 3 checkboxes feature a smooth animation and clear visual states for checked, unchecked, and indeterminate.

@cssprop.text: --checkbox-state-layer-size: Size of the state layer for touch target.

@fires.text: focus - Dispatched when the checkbox receives focus.

members.description: If true, displays the checkbox in an indeterminate state.

members.description: If true, the checkbox has rounded corners. Defaults to `false`.

The checkbox size.
Possible values are: `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the checkbox. Defaults to `false`.

@summary.text: A Monaco-based code editing component with syntax highlighting and theming.

@overview.text:
- CodeEditor wraps Monaco Editor as a web component.
 - Supports JS/JSON/HTML languages, read-only mode, line numbers, minimap, and dark/light theme.
 - Emits
`change`
when content is edited and updates value from property changes.

@example.text:
```html
<wc-code-editor
  language="javascript"
  style="width: 100%; --code-editor-height: 10rem;"
  value="function hello() { console.log('Hello'); }"
  lineNumbers="on"
  minimap="false">
</wc-code-editor>
```

customElement.name: CodeEditor
customElement.summary: A Monaco-based code editing component with syntax highlighting and theming.

@overview.text:

Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: softDisabled
members.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button and the button is visually styled as disabled. But the button is still focusable. Defaults to `false`.

members.name: disabledReason
members.description: If button is disabled, the reason why it is disabled.

members.name: href
members.description: Hyperlink to navigate to on click.

members.name: target
members.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

members.name: throttleDelay
members.description: Sets the delay for throttle in milliseconds. Defaults to 200 milliseconds.

members.name: isPressed
members.description: States

events.description: Dispatched when the button is clicked.
events.name: button:click

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: Type is preset of color and variant. Type will be only applied.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description:
The visual style of the button.

 Possible variant values:
`"filled"` is a filled button.
`"outlined"` is an outlined button.
`"text"` is a transparent button.
`"tonal"` is a light color button.
`"elevated"` is elevated button

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Defines the primary color of the button. This can be set to predefined color names to apply specific color themes.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
Button size.
Possible values are `"sm"`, `"md"`, `"lg"`. Defaults to `"md"`.

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button. Defaults to `false`.

attributes.name: soft-disabled
attributes.description: If true, the user cannot interact with the button and the button is visually styled as disabled. But the button is still focusable. Defaults to `false`.

attributes.name: disabled-reason
attributes.description: If button is disabled, the reason why it is disabled.

attributes.name: href
attributes.description: Hyperlink to navigate to on click.

attributes.name: target
attributes.description: Sets or retrieves the window or frame at which to target content.

attributes.name: throttleDelay
attributes.description: Sets the delay for throttle in milliseconds. Defaults to 200 milliseconds.

customElement.summary: Icon buttons allow users to take actions, and make choices, with a single tap.

@summary.text: Provides ripple effect for interactive elements.

@overview.text: Ripple creates a visual feedback effect when users interact with buttons or other clickable elements.

customElement.name: Ripple

members.name: disabled
members.description: Disables the ripple.

members.name: shouldReactToEvent
members.description:
Returns `true` if
 - the ripple element is enabled
 - the pointer is primary for the input type
 - the pointer is the pointer that started the interaction, or will start
the interaction
 - the pointer is a touch, or the pointer state has the primary button
held, or the pointer is hovering

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description: Disables the ripple.

customElement.summary: Provides ripple effect for interactive elements.

@summary.text: A configurable component for displaying tabular data.

@overview.text: The table component displays rows of data with support for sorting, pagination, row selection, and fixed columns.

@fires.text: page - Dispatched when the page or page size changes.

customElement.name: Table

members.name: columns
members.description:
Grid columns configuration.
Each column can have: name, label, width (px), fixed (boolean), template (function).

members.name: data
members.description: Grid data to display on table.

members.name: selectionType
members.description: Row selection type. Set to `"checkbox"` to enable checkbox selection.

members.name: selectedRowKeys
members.description: Array of selected row key values.

members.name: keyField
members.description: The field name used as the unique key for each row. Defaults to `"id"`.

members.name: managed
members.description: If true, sorting and pagination are managed externally (controlled mode).

members.name: sortable
members.description: If true, columns are sortable. Defaults to `true`.

members.name: sortBy
members.description: The field name currently used for sorting.

members.name: sortOrder
members.description: The current sort order. Possible values are `"asc"` and `"desc"`. Defaults to `"asc"`.

members.name: paginate
members.description: If true, pagination is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

members.name: page
members.description: The current page number (1-based). Defaults to `1`.

members.name: pageSize
members.description: The number of rows per page. Defaults to `10`.

members.name: totalItems
members.description: Total number of items (used in managed/controlled mode).

members.name: emptyStateHeadline
members.description: Headline text shown when the table has no data.

members.name: emptyStateDescription
members.description: Description text shown when the table has no data.

events.name: selection-change
events.description: Dispatched when the row selection changes.

events.name: cell-click
events.description: Dispatched when a table cell is clicked.

events.name: sort
events.description: Dispatched when the table is sorted.

events.name: page
events.description: Dispatched when the page or page size changes.

attributes.name: columns
attributes.description:
Grid columns configuration.
Each column can have: name, label, width (px), fixed (boolean), template (function).

attributes.description: Grid data to display on table.

attributes.name: key-field
attributes.description: The field name used as the unique key for each row. Defaults to `"id"`.

attributes.name: managed
attributes.description: If true, sorting and pagination are managed externally (controlled mode).

attributes.name: sortable
attributes.description: If true, columns are sortable. Defaults to `true`.

attributes.name: sort-by
attributes.description: The field name currently used for sorting.

attributes.name: sort-order
attributes.description: The current sort order. Possible values are `"asc"` and `"desc"`. Defaults to `"asc"`.

attributes.name: paginate
attributes.description: If true, pagination is enabled. Defaults to `true`.

attributes.name: page
attributes.description: The current page number (1-based). Defaults to `1`.

attributes.name: page-size
attributes.description: The number of rows per page. Defaults to `10`.

@summary.text: A tree node represents a single item in a hierarchical tree structure. It supports nesting, icons, links, and keyboard navigation.

members.description: The nesting depth level (set automatically by the parent tree-view).
